title: Building Simple Systems
excerpt: Why I like keeping projects small, understandable, and easy to maintain.
bodyHtml:
I try to keep my workflow simple on purpose.

Small systems are easier to understand, easier to change, and easier to trust.

What I look for

  • clear inputs
  • a small number of steps
  • obvious outcomes

The goal

The goal is not to have the most advanced system. The goal is to have one I can keep using.

title: A Cleaner Workflow for Focus
excerpt: A few habits and tools that help me stay focused when I’m juggling many tasks.
bodyHtml:
Focus is easier when the environment is quiet.

That means fewer tabs, fewer lists, and fewer decisions.

My basics

  • a short task list
  • one place for notes
  • a fixed time to review what matters

title: Notes on Learning in Public
excerpt: How sharing work as you go can make the process more useful for both you and your readers.
bodyHtml:
Writing while I’m learning helps me notice what I actually understand.

It also makes it easier for other people to follow the process.

Why it helps

  1. It creates a record.
  2. It surfaces gaps quickly.
  3. It makes progress visible.
